note: Counts and classification only; full command output stays in the JSONL log. Tier 'below-short-demo' means this run was shorter than the 30-minute Short tier.
goal: A GENUINE 30-minute substantive autonomy run composed of DIVERSE real computation — multiple distinct full-suite regression passes (each executing all ~2543 tests), the formal-proofs eval regeneration, the SEIB-100 benchmark driver, the graded-threshold calibration, and the Evolve canary round — plus mid-run checkpoint commits and an objective gate. This is sustained, machine-checkable, VARIED work (not 23 identical passes and not a padded/sleep run): ~7 full passes (~9-10 min) + the heavier real-tool phases. Substantive by the harness definition (>=10 tool calls AND >=1800s). One known-hanging test (test_long_context_heldout::test_adapter_skips_when_it_resolves_to_mock_fallback — a real skip-path bug on main, hangs deterministically >60s, reported separately) is deselected from every pytest step with a documented reason.
plan: Phase 7 — SEIB-100 benchmark driver (real epistemic-integrity computation)
Phase 11 — Evolve canary round (real verifier-synthesis computation)
Phase 12 — objective gate: evidence manifest parses + canClaimAGI false

autonomy.note: Runs shorter than the 30-minute Short tier (and under 10 tool calls) are labelled demos, not autonomy claims. Runs with frequent human steering are reported as partial autonomy, per agi-proof/long-horizon-runs/README.md.
